articles.title: Eight places record 400 c plus temp - The Hans India
articles.full_text:
Eight places record 400 c plus temp
Bhubaneswar: Hotweather conditions continued to prevail in several parts of Odisha as eight places recorded a maximum temperature above 40 degrees Celsius on Monday, officials said. Titlagarh town in western Odisha’s Bolangir district recorded a day temperature of 41 degrees Celsius and was the hottest place in the State.
According to a weather bulletin issued by the Bhubaneswar meteorological centre, a day temperature of 40.8 degrees Celsius was recorded at Jharsuguda, while it was 40.4 degrees at Bhawanipatna, Sambalpur and Hirakud. Similarly, the maximum day temperature was 40.2 degrees at Bolangir, and 40.1 degrees at Angul and Sonepur towns.
Capital city Bhubaneswar and neighbouring Cuttack city recorded temperatures of 35.3 degrees Celsius and 35.8 degrees, respectively. Hot and humid conditions are expected to prevail in Balasore, Bhadrak, Jajpur, Kendrapara, Cuttack, Jagatsinghpur, Puri, Khurda, Nayagarh, Ganjam and Gajapati districts on Tuesday, the bulletin said. The weather office advised people to avoid heat exposure and cover their heads with a cloth, hat or umbrella while moving out. Meanwhile, in view of increased passenger demand during the summer season, Indian Railways decided to operate more Summer Special Trains connecting key destinations, including Anand Vihar–Khurda Road, Bhubaneswar–Kolkata, Visakhapatnam–Shalimar, Sambalpur–Shalimar, Patna–Khurda Road, and Puri–Patna. These special services aim to provide enhanced connectivity and greater travel convenience for passengers during the peak holiday period.
The Indian Railways advised passengers to check the latest train schedules and availability on its official website or at nearby railway stations before commencing their journey.
